description: Research corpus grounding the taktology takt-planning vocabulary. Built via the research-grounded-agents skill (discovery sweep, 2026-06-23). Every source has a verified URL; design-driving sources trace to decisions in research/decisions/.
verification_note: URLs and bibliographic facts cross-checked via web search and Crossref; fields marked verified:false or verified:"partial" still need a manual confirmation before formal citation. No PDFs are committed (license-respecting); open-access mirrors are linked where the publisher copy is paywalled.

clusters.takt-foundations: Takt planning theory, definitions, methodology, takt control/steering
clusters.location-based: LBMS / line-of-balance / flowline and its relationship to takt
clusters.takt-bim-automation: Takt + BIM integration and automated takt-plan / zone generation
clusters.ifc-ontology-graph: IFC process schema, ontologies (ifcOWL, BOT, DTC), topology & property-graph layer
clusters.implementation-cases: Empirical case studies, LPS integration, takt control & digital monitoring tools
clusters.bim-takt-breakdown: BIM-integrated takt planning, spatio-temporal / work breakdown structures, and Total BIM single-source-of-truth (Chalmers construction-visualization group)

sources.title: BIMTakt – Optimierung der Produktionsplanung durch ganzheitliche Integration von BIM und Lean Construction
sources.authors: Becker, S.; Tschickardt, T.
sources.note: CORPUS ANCHOR — the paper this whole project starts from. Crossref records OA (zero embargo); confirm the OA PDF download manually (Wiley HTML returned 402 to automated fetch).

sources.title: History and Theoretical Foundations of Takt Planning and Takt Control
sources.authors: Haghsheno, S.; Binninger, M.; Dlouhy, J.; Sterlike, S.
sources.note: Cluster A anchor (definitions & origins). Traces 'takt' from stationary production to construction; cites Bulhoes et al. 2005 as first IGLC use.

sources.title: Three-Level Method of Takt Planning and Takt Control – A New Approach for Designing Production Systems in Construction
sources.authors: Dlouhy, J.; Binninger, M.; Oprach, S.; Haghsheno, S.
sources.note: Macro/normal/micro takt-zone hierarchy. Directly relevant to layering TaktZone.

sources.title: Technical Takt Planning and Takt Control in Construction
sources.authors: Binninger, M.; Dlouhy, J.; Haghsheno, S.
sources.note: Clearest English statement of the planning-vs-control (Taktplanung/Taktsteuerung) split.

sources.title: Takt Time Planning for Construction of Exterior Cladding
sources.authors: Frandson, A.; Berghede, K.; Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: Paper ID confirmed = 902 (an agent's alternative 910 was wrong). Origin of the practical zone/cycle vocabulary.

sources.title: Development of a Takt-Time Plan: A Case Study
sources.authors: Frandson, A.; Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: The canonical six-step takt-planning procedure — maps onto the generation loop.

sources.title: Collaborative Takt Time Planning of Non-Repetitive Work
sources.authors: Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: Introduces 'work density' — conference origin of the 2022 journal method.

sources.title: Work Density Method for Takt Planning of Construction Processes with Nonrepetitive Work
sources.authors: Tommelein, I. D.
sources.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE), 148(12), 04022134
sources.note: Article number 04022134 confirmed. Formal work-density / zone-balancing method; basis for quantity->duration and actsOn.

sources.title: Location-Based Management for Construction: Planning, Scheduling and Control
sources.authors: Kenley, R.; Seppänen, O.
sources.note: Cluster B anchor. The LBMS / Location Breakdown Structure / flowline reference; the paradigm takt is contrasted against.

sources.title: Location-Based Management of Construction Projects: Part of a New Typology for Project Scheduling Methodologies
sources.authors: Kenley, R.; Seppänen, O.
sources.venue: Conference paper (proceedings unverified — possibly IGLC-14 Santiago 2006)
sources.note: Venue/proceedings unconfirmed. Scheduling-methodology taxonomy positioning location-based methods vs CPM.

sources.title: Comparison Between Location Based Management and Takt Time Planning
sources.authors: Frandson, A. G.; Seppänen, O.; Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: The key paper disambiguating LBMS from takt. Scopes what taktology is and is not.

sources.title: The Combination of Last Planner System and Location-Based Management System
sources.authors: Seppänen, O.; Ballard, G.; Pesonen, S.
sources.note: LBMS + LPS complementarity; genealogy for takt + LPS integration.

sources.title: Integrated Forecasting Approach for Takt and Location-Based Scheduling
sources.authors: Seppänen, O.; et al. (co-authors unverified)
sources.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE), 151(12), 04025199
sources.note: Co-author list unverified. Most recent source integrating takt and location-based control.

sources.title: BIM-based Takt-Time Planning and Takt Control: Requirements for Digital Construction Process Management
sources.authors: Melzner, J.
sources.venue: ISARC 2019 (36th Int. Symposium on Automation and Robotics in Construction), IAARC
sources.note: Requirements framing for BIM-driven takt; not an automation algorithm.

sources.title: Workload leveling based on work space zoning for takt planning
sources.authors: Jabbari, A.; Tommelein, I. D.; Kaminsky, P. M.
sources.note: WoLZo: optimization to derive takt zones by leveling work density. Operates on work-density abstraction, not raw geometry.

sources.title: BIM-Based Combination of Takt Time and Discrete Event Simulation for Implementing Just in Time in Construction Scheduling under Constraints
sources.authors: Abbasi, S.; Taghizade, K.; Noorzai, E.
sources.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE), 146(12)
sources.note: Couples takt time + 4D BIM + discrete-event simulation for JIT scheduling.

sources.title: Method for automating the processes of generating and using 4D BIM models integrated with location-based planning and Last Planner System
sources.authors: Silveira, B. F.; Costa, D. B.
sources.note: Automates 4D BIM generation linked to LBMS + LPS (not strictly takt).

sources.title: The Creation of Construction Schedules in 4D BIM: A Comparison of Conventional and Automated Approaches
sources.authors: Doukari, O.; Seck, B.; Greenwood, D.
sources.note: Baseline for automated vs manual 4D schedule generation (generic, not takt).

sources.title: An Integrated BIM–NLP Framework for Design-Informed Automated Construction Schedule Generation
sources.authors: Donia, M.; Elbeltagi, E.; Elhakeem, A.; Wefki, H.
sources.note: BIM+NLP automated activity/duration/sequence generation; relevant by analogy to takt-plan automation.

sources.title: EXPRESS to OWL for construction industry: Towards a recommendable and usable ifcOWL ontology
sources.authors: Pauwels, P.; Terkaj, W.
sources.note: Defines ifcOWL. Grounds ADR-3 (why NOT import ifcOWL — full-schema mirror, heavyweight).

sources.title: ifcOWL (official buildingSMART ifcOWL ontology page)
sources.authors: buildingSMART International (Linked Data Working Group)
sources.note: Authoritative ifcOWL artifact page (IFC 2x3–4.0).

sources.title: BOT: The Building Topology Ontology of the W3C Linked Building Data Group
sources.authors: Rasmussen, M. H.; Lefrançois, M.; Schneider, G. F.; Pauwels, P.
sources.note: Cluster D anchor. The minimal topology ontology taktology's spatial layer rests on (via top:).

sources.title: BOT – Building Topology Ontology (specification / namespace)
sources.authors: W3C Linked Building Data Community Group (eds.)
sources.note: Live dereferenceable BOT namespace the model aligns to.

sources.title: IFC 4.3 – IfcProcessExtension (IfcTask, IfcRelSequence, IfcWorkSchedule, IfcTaskTime, …)
sources.authors: buildingSMART International
sources.note: The normative process/scheduling schema — the skos:closeMatch targets in takt.ttl.

sources.title: Advanced Process Representation for Semi-Automated Linking between Construction Schedules and IFC Files
sources.authors: Schlenger, J.; Borrmann, A.
sources.venue: LDAC 2024 (Linked Data in Architecture and Construction); CEUR-WS Vol. 3824, 36–49
sources.note: Minimal schedule-process ontology + schedule-to-IFC linking. Closest existing work to taktology's process layer — evaluate for reuse.

sources.title: Reference architecture and ontology framework for Digital Twin Construction
sources.authors: Schlenger, J.; Pluta, K.; Mathew, A.; Yeung, T.; Sacks, R.; Borrmann, A.
sources.note: Exact autcon DOI/volume unverified. Plan-vs-actual multi-graph architecture; informs how planned/as-built takt data coexist.

sources.title: Digital Twin Construction (DTC) Ontology (specification)
sources.authors: Schlenger, J.; Yeung, T.; Bus, N.; Sacks, R.; Borrmann, A.; et al.
sources.note: ADOPTED — taktology v0.5.0 aligns to DTC v2 (docs ADR-12; supersedes the v0.3.x-v0.4.x v1 alignment). VERSION RESOLVED + VERIFIED FULL 2026-07-01 by reading both published TTLs: v1 (namespace .../ontology#, modified 2023-11-00) and v2 (namespace .../ontology/v2#, modified 2024-07-31; imports BOT 0.3.2 + GeoSPARQL 1.1 + BuildingElement). v2 drops WorkPackage/Activity/Task/hasActivity/isLocatedIn and restructures the backbone as Process -> AsPlannedProcess/AsPerformedProcess. v0.5.0 parents: TaktTask<=AsPlannedProcess, TaktZone<=AsPlannedWorkingZone, Crew<=AsPlannedWorkerCrew, TaktGraph<=ConstructionSchedule, performedIn<=isPerformedIn, actsOn<=hasTarget. Pinned with sha256 in ontology/alignments.lock.json.

sources.title: Topologic: A toolkit for spatial and topological modelling
sources.authors: Jabi, W.; Aish, R.; Lannon, S.; Chatzivasileiadi, A.; Wardhana, N. M.
sources.note: Citable foundation for Topologic/TopologicPy — the non-manifold spatial-graph engine under top:. Grounds ADR-1.

sources.title: TopologicPy ontology and documentation (top:)
sources.authors: Jabi, W. (TopologicPy project)
sources.note: RE-VERIFIED FULL 2026-07-01 against the published TTL: v0.2.0 (issued 2026-05-24, modified 2026-06-27), canonical IRI http://w3id.org/topologicpy, namespace http://w3id.org/topologicpy# (resolves via w3id). v0.2.0 adds the graph layer (top:Graph + subclasses incl. top:KnowledgeGraph; top:Node/top:Relationship/top:hasPredicate) that ADR-15 types takt:TaktGraph with. Published TTL declares NO FunctionalZone and NO top:area/top:volume (the in-code Ontology.py keeps them as compatibility terms). Pinned with sha256 in ontology/alignments.lock.json. No peer-reviewed ontology paper exists; cite the TTL + software docs (topologicpy.readthedocs.io).

sources.title: Revealing the internal structure of IFC-Graph for efficient querying and knowledge discovery
sources.authors: Zhu, J.; Nisbet, N.; Kang, R.; Wen, Y.; Wang, M.; Brilakis, I.
sources.note: Exact DOI unverified. IFC-as-labelled-property-graph (LPG/Neo4j) design — the property-graph branch vs RDF.

sources.title: Takt-Time Planning and the Last Planner
sources.authors: Frandson, A.; Berghede, K.; Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: How takt (production-system design) and LPS (collaborative control) integrate.

sources.title: Takt Time Planning of Interiors on a Pre-Cast Hospital Project
sources.authors: Frandson, A. G.; Tommelein, I. D.
sources.note: Interior fit-out case + auto-generation of daily space schedules (workspace congestion).

sources.title: Implementing Takt Planning and Takt Control Into Residential Construction
sources.authors: Lehtovaara, J.; Mustonen, I.; Peuronen, P.; Seppänen, O.; Peltokorpi, A.
sources.note: Residential interior-phase empirical pilot of takt planning + control.

sources.title: How Takt Production Contributes to Construction Production Flow: A Theoretical Model
sources.authors: Lehtovaara, J.; Seppänen, O.; Peltokorpi, A.; Kujansuu, P.; Grönvall, M.
sources.note: Flow theory: distinguishes location flow vs trade flow — conceptual frame for what taktology optimizes.

sources.title: Takt Production Monitoring and Control in Apartment Renovation Projects
sources.authors: Keskiniva, K.; Saari, A.; Junnonen, J.-M.
sources.note: Author order read from search metadata. Digital takt monitoring/control tool; renovation case.

sources.title: Application of Takt and Kanban to Modular Wind Turbine Construction
sources.authors: Lerche, J.; Neve, H. H.; Wandahl, S.; et al.
sources.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE), 148(2)
sources.note: Full author list unverified. Takt + Kanban + PDCA outside buildings (offshore wind).

sources.title: Visual Workload Leveling and Zoning Using Work Density Method for Construction Process Planning
sources.authors: Singh, V. V.; Tommelein, I. D. (verify)
sources.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE), 149(10)
sources.note: Companion to the 2022 WDM paper; visual workload leveling / zoning. Authors to confirm.

sources.title: Structuring Construction Projects for Integrated Delivery
sources.authors: Ljung, E. (Chalmers / Skanska)
sources.venue: Licentiate thesis, Chalmers University of Technology (Architecture and Civil Engineering)
sources.note: USER-SUPPLIED SEED. Introduces a Spatio-Temporal Breakdown Structure (TBS) unifying space + time for flow/takt production — directly parallels taktology's TaktZone (spatial) x TaktTime (temporal) core. Design Science across two projects; ties to Total BIM.

sources.title: Identifying and Developing Prerequisites for Takt Planning in a BIM-Based Construction Process
sources.authors: Ljung, E. (Skanska); Viklund Tallgren, M.; Roupé, M.; Johansson, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: CONVR 2023 (23rd Int. Conf. on Construction Applications of Virtual Reality), Florence
sources.note: USER-SUPPLIED SEED. Prerequisites for integrating BIM + takt across phases (focus groups -> design workshops -> site evaluation); a shared breakdown (WBS) as the holistic denominator.

sources.title: Developing support for BIM-based takt time schedules for production control
sources.authors: Viklund Tallgren, M.; Johansson, M.; Roupé, M. (Chalmers); Ljung, E. (Skanska)
sources.venue: CONVR 2022 (22nd Int. Conf. on Construction Applications of Virtual Reality), Seoul, 723–730
sources.note: Three cases on obstacles to BIM+takt; argues for direct links between takt schedules and model objects via a collaborative planning system — supports the schedule<->element (actsOn/performedIn) design.

sources.title: BIM-tool development enhancing collaborative scheduling for pre-construction
sources.authors: Viklund Tallgren, M.; Roupé, M.; Johansson, M.; Bosch-Sijtsema, P. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: Journal of Information Technology in Construction (ITcon), 25, 374–397
sources.note: Peer-reviewed BIM-integrated collaborative scheduling tool for pre-construction; the data model (activities bound to BIM objects) a takt ontology extends with zones + takt times.

sources.title: Collaborative production planning with BIM – Design, development and evaluation of a Virtual Production Planning system
sources.authors: Viklund Tallgren, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: Doctoral thesis, Chalmers University of Technology (ISBN 978-91-7905-581-3)
sources.note: Capstone PhD: a Virtual Production Planning (VPP) system for collaborative, BIM-linked scheduling (individual / co-located / 4D-VR). Background substrate for the group's takt work.

sources.title: 4D modelling using virtual collaborative planning and scheduling
sources.authors: Viklund Tallgren, M.; Roupé, M.; Johansson, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: Journal of Information Technology in Construction (ITcon), 26, 763–782
sources.note: Social co-creation of 4D schedules in multi-user VR — couples activities to time + 3D space (the 4D core a zone-time takt model formalizes).

sources.title: Developing a collaborative planning tool for construction – A BIM-enhanced planning and scheduling tool for production
sources.authors: Viklund Tallgren, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: Licentiate thesis, Chalmers University of Technology
sources.note: Precursor to the 2021 PhD; frames planning as sociotechnical and motivates BIM integration. Full-text PDF link was ambiguous in discovery — use the Chalmers page as canonical.

sources.title: Total BIM on the construction site: a dynamic single source of information
sources.authors: Disney, O.; Roupé, M.; Johansson, M. (Chalmers); Ris, J.; Höglin, P. (Byggstyrning)
sources.venue: Journal of Information Technology in Construction (ITcon), 28, 519–538 (CONVR special issue)
sources.note: Co-authored by Byggstyrning (this repo's organization). Total BIM = the model is the legally-binding single source of truth, drawings abandoned — the on-site environment a takt/zone-time model would be consumed in.

sources.title: Embracing BIM in its totality: a Total BIM case study
sources.authors: Disney, O.; Roupé, M.; Johansson, M. (Chalmers); Domenico Leto, A. (Politecnico di Milano)
sources.note: Definitional Total BIM single-case (Swedish 'Celsius'): four success factors (legally-binding BIM, cloud model mgmt, mobile BIM software, leadership). Drawing-free delivery context.

sources.title: The Significance of a Shared Breakdown Structure for Work Preparation Planning for a Predictable Execution
sources.authors: Ljung, E.; Viklund Tallgren, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: Creative Construction Conference 2024 (CCC2024), Prague, paper CCC2024-159
sources.note: Paper III of Ljung's TBS licentiate. Full title CONFIRMED via the licentiate's appended-papers list (includes 'a Shared Breakdown Structure' — the Chalmers record used a shortened form). Shared breakdown across disciplines/documents increases traceability/predictability (two rooftop-assembly cases). OA status still unconfirmed.

sources.title: Can Interoperability Between Disciplines Be Improved by Adding Phasing Information Using Standardised Coding?
sources.authors: Ljung, E.; Viklund Tallgren, M. (Chalmers)
sources.venue: ARCOM 2024 (40th Annual Conf. of the Assoc. of Researchers in Construction Management), eds. Thomson & Neilson, London, 111–120
sources.note: Paper IV of Ljung's TBS licentiate; authors/venue/editors (Thomson & Neilson) CONFIRMED via the licentiate's appended-papers list. Proposes ISO/IEC 81346 reference-designation coding to add phasing info linking disciplines — relevant to a stable zone/phase IDENTIFIER scheme for interoperable takt zones. No DOI (ISBN-only proceedings); OA unconfirmed.
